message:
fix(packs): close quote-bypass on rm -rf / and git add .

Two related quote-bypass issues where the shell unquotes a single/double
quoted argument to the same value but the regex did not:

1. core/filesystem `rm-rf-root-home`: `rm -rf "/"`, `rm -rf '/'`,
   `rm -rf "~/"` were not matched because `\s+[/~]` requires `/` or `~`
   immediately after whitespace, but quoted forms had `"` or `'` in
   between. These are Critical commands — missing the quoted form is
   the worst possible failure mode. Added optional `['"]?` before `[/~]`.

2. strict_git `add-all-dot`: `git add '.'`, `git add "."` bypassed the
   `git add .` block for the same reason — quoted `.` evaluates to
   literal `.` at the shell level, staging everything identically. Added
   optional `['"]?` around the literal `.`.

Regression tests added for both. 2178 tests passing.
